Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Patient admitted for HIV-related conditi: If a patient is admitted for an HIV-related condition, the principal diagnosis should be B20, Human immunodeficiency virus [HIV] disease followed by additional diagnosis codes for all reported HIV-related conditions. Exception: If the reason for admission is hemolytic-uremic syndrome associated with HIV disease, assign code D59.31 followed by B20.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Patient with HIV disease admitted for un: If a patient with HIV disease is admitted for an unrelated condition, the unrelated condition should be principal diagnosis. B20 and other HIV-related conditions should be listed as additional diagnoses.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Whether the patient is newly diagnosed: New or previous diagnosis does not affect sequencing decision.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Asymptomatic human immunodeficiency viru: Z21 is used when patient is HIV positive but asymptomatic. Do not use Z21 if AIDS, HIV disease, or HIV-related illness is documented; use B20 instead.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Patients with inconclusive HIV serology: Assign R75 when serology is inconclusive with no definitive diagnosis or manifestations.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Previously diagnosed HIV-related illness: Once diagnosed with an HIV-related illness, always code B20 in subsequent encounters. Never assign R75 or Z21 in these cases.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.HIV Infection in Pregnancy, Childbirth a: Principal code O98.7- for HIV illness complicating pregnancy, followed by B20 and related illness codes. For asymptomatic HIV in pregnancy, use O98.7- and Z21.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Encounters for testing for HIV: Z11.4 for screening. Code signs/symptoms if present. Z71.7 for counseling. Negative results use Z71.7; positive results follow HIV guidelines.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.HIV managed by antiretroviral medication: B20 for HIV disease, plus Z79.899 for long-term antiretroviral use.
Human Immunodeficiency Virus Infections.Encounter for HIV Prophylaxis Measures: Z29.81 for HIV pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP), plus codes for risk factors.

Certain Infectious and Parasitic Disease.Infectious agents as the cause of diseas: Use additional codes from B95-B97 to identify organism when infection code does not specify it.
Certain Infectious and Parasitic Disease.Infections resistant to antibiotics: Assign Z16 code for resistance after infection code if resistance is not identified there.

Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Negative or inconclusive blood cultures: Negative cultures do not rule out sepsis if clinically evident.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Sepsis with organ dysfunction: Follow severe sepsis coding if organ dysfunction present.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Acute organ dysfunction not clearly asso: Do not code as severe sepsis unless linked; query if unclear.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Severe sepsis: Requires code for infection, R65.2, and organ dysfunction code(s). Query if unclear.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Septic shock: Sequence systemic infection first, then R65.21 or T81.12. Include other organ dysfunction codes.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Sepsis or severe sepsis with a localized: If sepsis reason for admission, code systemic infection first, then localized infection. Reverse if localized infection first.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Sepsis due to a postprocedural infection: Code surgical wound infection first, then T81.44 or O86.04, plus infectious agent. Add R65.2 if severe.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Postprocedural infection and postprocedu: Use T81.12 after infection codes; do not assign R65.21.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Sepsis and severe sepsis associated with: Sequence based on principal diagnosis definition. Use only one R65 code; assign R65.2 if severe sepsis results.
Sepsis, Severe Sepsis, and Septic Shock.Hemolytic-uremic syndrome associated wit: D59.31 as principal, infection and other conditions secondary.

Selection and sequencing of MRSA codes.Combination codes for MRSA infection: Use combination code; do not add B95.62 or Z16.11.
Selection and sequencing of MRSA codes.Other codes for MRSA infection: Use condition code plus B95.62; do not use Z16.11.
Selection and sequencing of MRSA codes.Methicillin susceptible Staphylococcus a: Z22.322 for MRSA colonization; Z22.321 for MSSA colonization.
Selection and sequencing of MRSA codes.MRSA colonization and infection: Assign both Z22.322 and MRSA infection code.

Zika virus infections.Code only confirmed cases: Assign A92.5 for confirmed cases based on provider documentation. For suspected/possible/probable, code symptoms or Z20.821.

Acute respiratory manifestations of COVI.Acute bronchitis: U07.1 + J20.8; use J40 if NOS
Acute respiratory manifestations of COVI.Lower respiratory infection: U07.1 + J22; use J98.8 if NOS respiratory infection

Coronavirus infections.Exposure to COVID-19: Z20.822 for exposure (asymptomatic or symptomatic with ruled-out infection).
Coronavirus infections.Signs and symptoms without definitive di: Code each symptom (e.g., R05.1, R05.9, R06.02, R50.9). Add Z20.822 if exposure.
Coronavirus infections.Follow-up visits after COVID-19 infectio: Z09 + Z86.16 if no residual symptoms; see other guidelines if symptoms present.
Coronavirus infections.Encounter for antibody testing: Z01.84 unless testing to confirm active infection or as follow-up.
